message:
fix(http): correct Connection header overwrite and H2 settings emission

Two protocol-level fixes in the HTTP stack:

1. H1 Connection header (server.rs): add_connection_close() now overwrites
   an existing Connection header value to "close" instead of silently
   skipping when a header already exists. Previously, a stale
   "Connection: keep-alive" would persist even when the server intended
   to close the connection, causing clients to reuse a closing socket.

2. H2 SETTINGS frame (settings.rs): to_settings_minimal_for_role() now
   compares against RFC 7540 spec defaults (u32::MAX for unlimited
   streams/header size) rather than the library's own struct defaults
   (256/65536). This ensures max_concurrent_streams and
   max_header_list_size are always emitted in the SETTINGS frame since
   they differ from the RFC's "unlimited" default that peers assume.
   Test updated to expect 3 settings instead of 2.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
